title: tephrosia interrupta
intro: Tephrosia interrupta, also known as the interrupted tephrosia or wild bean, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tephrosia interrupta include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional remedies and pharmaceuticals.
